completed_slice: Defined the immutable repository coverage snapshot required before a provenance search can support unlocated_after_exhaustive_coverage.
finding: Exhaustive provenance classifications are valid only relative to a retained, replayable ref-and-object snapshot. Repository scope expansion reopens current effective classification while preserving prior snapshot-qualified observations.

verification.reason: This slice is a provenance contract; the executable manifest builder remains the next implementation step.

claims.observed: No retained authenticated all-accessible-refs snapshot was available in this run.
claims.inferred: Commands and timestamps alone are insufficient to reproduce an exhaustive search after refs move.
claims.proposed: Build a canonical snapshot manifest and digest-bound result packet.
claims.superseded: Repeated bounded searches do not become exhaustive through repetition alone.
claims.unresolved: M-004, M-005, and M-006 remain unresolved pending exhaustive snapshot-bound traversal.

next_action: Implement a fail-closed snapshot-manifest builder that canonicalizes accessible refs and target object IDs, records traversal/query contracts and blind spots, computes coverage_snapshot_id, and emits a result-set digest for M-004 through M-006.
privacy_boundary: Public repository metadata and abstract provenance rules only; no private chat, health, credentials, personal data, or unreviewed uploads.
specialist_registry_change: none; existing continuity specialists cover the work and no reusable capability gap was demonstrated.
